LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT FRIENDSHIP HOUSE ASSOCIATION OF AMERICAN INDIANS INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - FRIENDSHIP HOUSE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT HE WAS TERMINATED AS A RESULT OF HIS MARITAL STATUS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THERE WAS NO DISCRIMINATION. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE IS DENIED BECAUSE THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THE CONTINUED DEPOSITION OF MS. WAUKAZOO WOULD LEAD TO FACTS THAT COULD CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: IRENE L. TOFFT, CSR #12913. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-18-2015 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Orlando Nakai To Produce Documents In Accordance With His Response To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-18-2015 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication By Defendant Friendship House Association Of American Indians Inc
Off Calendar Dec-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John McMorrow of Law Offices of John B. McMorrow telephone number 510-651-9961 for Plaintiff.
Aaron Hayes and Julia Marquis telephone number 415-689-1227 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FRIENDSHIP HOUSE ASSOCIATION OF AMERICAN INDIANS INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication - Friendship House's motion for summary judgment is granted. The motion is granted as to the first and second causes of action because plaintiff does not demonstrate that he was terminated as a result of his marital status. Plaintiff was terminated based on the allegation that he had a gun and was angry. The undisputed evidence shows that plaintiff worked for defendant for 14 years while plaintiff was married to Ms. Nakai. If marital status were an issue, plaintiff would have been terminated earlier. It was the identity of Ms. Nakai-not the marital status-that led to the discharge. (Chen v. County of Orange (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 926). The motion is granted as to the third cause of action because there was no discrimination. In addition, Cotran v. Rollins Hudig Hall International (1998) 17 Cal.4th 93 does not apply to this case. The undisputed evidence shows that plaintiff was an at-will employee. "Cotran' expressly applies only to cases in which an employee is under an "implied agreement not to be dismissed except for 'good cause' ...." (Halvorsen v. Aramark Uniform Services, Inc. (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 1383, 1391). Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied because there is no showing that the continued deposition of Ms. Waukazoo would lead to facts that could create a triable issue of material fact. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR #12913. (302/HK)

PLAINTIFF ORLANDO NAKAI's Motion For An Order Permitting Leave To File A First Amended Complaint is Granted. The parties stipulated to the relief sought by the motion.
